libraryDescription:
The VCSSL Text library is a standard library for text processing.

This library provides features such as obtaining lengths and substrings,searching and replacing, and splitting for string variables.Regular expressions can also be used in searching, replacing, and splitting.

variable0.name: ALL
variable0.description: The search mode for finding all parts matching a string.

variable1.name: ALL_PATTERN
variable1.description: The search mode for finding all parts matching a regular expression.

variable2.name: FIRST
variable2.description: The search mode for finding the first part matching a string.

variable3.name: FIRST_PATTERN
variable3.description: The search mode for finding the first part matching a regular expression.

variable4.name: LAST
variable4.description: The search mode for finding the last part matching a string.

variable5.name: LAST_PATTERN
variable5.description: The search mode for finding the last part matching a regular expression.

variable6.name: START
variable6.description: The checking mode for determining whether the beginning of a string matches a substring.

variable7.name: START_PATTERN
variable7.description: The checking mode for determining whether the beginning of a string matches a regular expression.

variable8.name: END
variable8.description: The checking mode for determining whether the end of a string matches a substring.

variable9.name: END_PATTERN
variable9.description: The checking mode for determining whether the end of a string matches a regular expression.

variable10.name: CONTAIN
variable10.description: The checking mode for determining whether any part of a string matches a substring.

variable11.name: CONTAIN_PATTERN
variable11.description: The checking mode for determining whether any part of a string matches a regular expression.

variable12.name: FULL_PATTERN
variable12.description: The checking mode for determining whether an entire string matches a regular expression.

variable13.name: TRIM
variable13.description: The mode of the adjustText function for trimming leading and trailing whitespace and line breaks.

variable14.name: LOWER_CASE
variable14.description: The mode of the adjustText function for converting uppercase alphabetic characters to lowercase.

variable15.name: UPPER_CASE
variable15.description: The mode of the adjustText function for converting lowercase alphabetic characters to uppercase.

function0.name: atText
function0.declaration: string atText( string text, int index )
function0.description: Returns the character at the specified index in a string as a one-character string value.
function0.parameters:
(string type) text : The target string. 
(int type) index : The character index.

function1.name: checkText
function1.declaration: bool checkText( string text, string query, int mode )
function1.description: Determines whether the beginning, end, or whole of a string matches the specified string or regular expression.
function1.parameters: (string type) text : The target text. (string type) query : The substring used for matching. (int type) mode : The checking mode ( START, END, CONTAIN, START_PATTERN, END_PATTERN, CONTAIN_PATTERN, FULL_PATTERN ).
function1.returnValue: (bool type) The result of the check (true if matched).

function2.name: adjustText
function2.declaration: string adjustText( string text, int mode )
function2.description:
Adjusts text in the specified mode.
Depending on the mode, leading and trailing whitespace and line breaks can be removed, or alphabetic characters can be unified to uppercase or lowercase.
function2.parameters: (string type) text : The target text. (int type) mode : The adjustment mode ( TRIM, LOWER_CASE, UPPER_CASE ).

function3.name: cropText
function3.declaration: string cropText( string text, int cropBegin, int cropEnd )
function3.description:
Returns the specified range extracted from a text.
If indices are assigned to the characters of the text starting from 0, the extracted range is the substring from cropBegin to cropEnd - 1.Also, if indices are assigned to the gaps between characters, the extracted range is exactly the part between the gap at cropBegin and the gap at cropEnd.
function3.parameters:
(string type) text : The target text. 
(int type) cropBegin : The starting position of the range to be extracted. 
(int type) cropEnd : The ending position of the range to be extracted.

function4.name: countText
function4.description: Counts and returns the number of characters in a text.
function4.returnValue: (int type) The number of characters in the text.

function5.name: countText
function5.declaration: int countText( string text, string query, int mode )
function5.description: Searches a text for parts matching a substring or regular expression, and returns the number of matches.
function5.parameters: (string type) text : The target text. (string type) query : The substring to search for. (int type) mode : The search mode ( ALL, FIRST, LAST, ALL_PATTERN, FIRST_PATTERN, LAST_PATTERN ).

function6.name: countText
function6.declaration: int countText( string text, string query, int searchBegin, int mode )
function6.description: Searches a text for parts matching a substring or regular expression from the specified position onward, and returns the number of matches.
function6.parameters: (string type) text : The target text. (string type) query : The substring to search for. (int type) searchBegin : The character index of the search starting position. (int type) mode : The search mode ( ALL, FIRST, LAST, ALL_PATTERN, FIRST_PATTERN, LAST_PATTERN ).

function7.name: countText
function7.declaration: int countText( string text, string query, int searchBegin, int searchEnd, int mode )
function7.description: Searches within the specified range of a text for parts matching a substring or regular expression, and returns the number of matches.
function7.parameters: (string type) text : The target text. (string type) query : The substring to search for. (int type) searchBegin : The character index of the search starting position. (int type) searchEnd : The character index of the search ending position plus 1 (see cropText). (int type) mode : The search mode ( ALL, FIRST, LAST, ALL_PATTERN, FIRST_PATTERN, LAST_PATTERN ).

function8.name: findText
function8.declaration: int[ ] findText( string text, string query, int mode )
function8.description: Searches a text for parts matching a substring or regular expression, and returns an array containing the character indices of the matched parts.
function8.parameters: (string type) text : The target text. (string type) query : The substring to search for. (int type) mode : The search mode ( ALL, FIRST, LAST, ALL_PATTERN, FIRST_PATTERN, LAST_PATTERN ).
function8.returnValue: (int[] type) An array storing the character indices of the matched parts.

function9.name: findText
function9.declaration: int[ ] findText( string text, string query, int searchBegin, int mode )
function9.description: Searches from the specified position onward in a text for parts matching a substring or regular expression, and returns an array containing the character indices of the matched parts.If no match is found, the return value is an array with zero elements.
function9.parameters: (string type) text : The target text. (string type) query : The substring to search for. (int type) searchBegin : The character index of the search starting position. (int type) mode : The search mode ( ALL, FIRST, LAST, ALL_PATTERN, FIRST_PATTERN, LAST_PATTERN ).
function9.returnValue: (int[] type) An array storing the character indices of the matched parts.

function10.name: findText
function10.declaration: int[ ] findText( string text, string query, int searchBegin, int searchEnd, int mode )
function10.description: Searches within the specified range of a text for parts matching a substring or regular expression, and returns an array containing the character indices of the matched parts.If no match is found, the return value is an array with zero elements.
function10.parameters: (string type) text : The target text. (string type) query : The substring to search for. (int type) searchBegin : The character index of the search starting position. (int type) searchEnd : The character index of the search ending position plus 1 (see cropText). (int type) mode : The search mode ( ALL, FIRST, LAST, ALL_PATTERN, FIRST_PATTERN, LAST_PATTERN ).
function10.returnValue: (int[] type) An array storing the character indices of the matched parts.

function11.name: extractText
function11.declaration: string[ ] extractText( string text, string query, int mode )
function11.description: Searches a text for parts matching a substring or regular expression, and returns an array containing the extracted matched strings.If no match is found, the return value is an array with zero elements.
function11.parameters: (string type) text : The target text. (string type) query : The substring to search for. (int type) mode : The search mode ( ALL, FIRST, LAST, ALL_PATTERN, FIRST_PATTERN, LAST_PATTERN ).
function11.returnValue: (string[] type) An array storing the extracted matched strings.

function12.name: extractText
function12.declaration: string[ ] extractText( string text, string query, int searchBegin, int mode )
function12.description:
Searches from the specified position onward in a text for parts matching a substring or regular expression, and returns an array containing the extracted matched strings.
If no match is found, the return value is an array with zero elements.
function12.parameters: (string type) text : The target text. (string type) query : The substring to search for. (int type) searchBegin : The character index of the search starting position. (int type) mode : The search mode ( ALL, FIRST, LAST, ALL_PATTERN, FIRST_PATTERN, LAST_PATTERN ).
function12.returnValue: (string[] type) An array storing the extracted matched strings.

function13.name: extractText
function13.declaration: string[ ] extractText( string text, string query, int searchBegin, int searchEnd, int mode )
function13.description:
Searches within the specified range of a text for parts matching a substring or regular expression, and returns an array containing the extracted matched strings.
If no match is found, the return value is an array with zero elements.
function13.parameters: (string type) text : The target text. (string type) query : The substring to search for. (int type) searchBegin : The character index of the search starting position. (int type) searchEnd : The character index of the search ending position plus 1 (see cropText). (int type) mode : The search mode ( ALL, FIRST, LAST, ALL_PATTERN, FIRST_PATTERN, LAST_PATTERN ).
function13.returnValue: (string[] type) An array storing the extracted matched strings.

function14.name: replaceText
function14.declaration: string replaceText( string text, string query, string newPart, int mode )
function14.description: Searches a text for parts matching a substring or regular expression, and returns the text with the matched parts replaced by another string.
function14.parameters: (string type) text : The target text. (string type) query : The substring to search for. (string type) newPart : The string to replace the matched parts with. (int type) mode : The search mode ( ALL, FIRST, LAST, ALL_PATTERN, FIRST_PATTERN, LAST_PATTERN ).
function14.returnValue: (string type) The text with the matched parts replaced.

function15.name: replaceText
function15.declaration: string replaceText( string text, string query, string newPart, int searchBegin, int mode )
function15.description: Searches from the specified position onward in a text for parts matching a substring or regular expression, and returns the text with the matched parts replaced by another string.
function15.parameters: (string type) text : The target text. (string type) query : The substring to search for. (string type) newPart : The string to replace the matched parts with. (int type) searchBegin : The search starting position. (int type) mode : The search mode ( ALL, FIRST, LAST, ALL_PATTERN, FIRST_PATTERN, LAST_PATTERN ).
function15.returnValue: (string type) The text with the matched parts replaced.

function16.name: replaceText
function16.declaration: string replaceText( string text, string query, string newPart, int searchBegin, int searchEnd, int mode )
function16.description: Searches within the specified range of a text for parts matching a substring or regular expression, and returns the text with the matched parts replaced by another string.
function16.parameters: (string type) text : The target text. (string type) query : The substring to search for. (string type) newPart : The string to replace the matched parts with. (int type) searchBegin : The character index of the search starting position. (int type) searchEnd : The character index of the search ending position plus 1 (see cropText). (int type) mode : The search mode ( ALL, FIRST, LAST, ALL_PATTERN, FIRST_PATTERN, LAST_PATTERN ).
function16.returnValue: (string type) The text with the matched parts replaced.

function17.name: splitText
function17.declaration: string[ ] splitText( string text, string query, int mode )
function17.description: Searches a text for parts matching a substring or regular expression, and returns an array containing the original text split at those matched parts.
function17.parameters: (string type) text : The target text. (string type) query : The substring to search for. (int type) mode : The search mode ( ALL, FIRST, LAST, ALL_PATTERN, FIRST_PATTERN, LAST_PATTERN ).
function17.returnValue: (string[] type) An array of text pieces split at the matched parts.

function18.name: splitText
function18.declaration: string[ ] splitText( string text, string query, int searchBegin, int mode )
function18.description: Searches from the specified position onward in a text for parts matching a substring or regular expression, and returns an array containing the original text split at those matched parts.
function18.parameters: (string type) text : The target text. (string type) query : The substring to search for. (int type) searchBegin : The character index of the search starting position. (int type) mode : The search mode ( ALL, FIRST, LAST, ALL_PATTERN, FIRST_PATTERN, LAST_PATTERN ).
function18.returnValue: (string[] type) An array of text pieces split at the matched parts.

function19.name: splitText
function19.declaration: string[ ] splitText( string text, string query, int searchBegin, int searchEnd, int mode )
function19.description: Searches within the specified range of a text for parts matching a substring or regular expression, and returns an array containing the original text split at those matched parts.
function19.parameters: (string type) text : The target text. (string type) query : The substring to search for. (int type) searchBegin : The character index of the search starting position. (int type) searchEnd : The character index of the search ending position plus 1 (see cropText). (int type) mode : The search mode ( ALL, FIRST, LAST, ALL_PATTERN, FIRST_PATTERN, LAST_PATTERN ).
function19.returnValue: (string[] type) An array of text pieces split at the matched parts.

function20.name: split
function20.declaration: string[ ] split( string text, string query )
function20.description:
Splits a string at positions matching a specific string, and returns the result as an array.
Currently, the newer splitText function is supported and provides the same or more advanced functionality.For example, splitText(text, query, ALL) gives the same result as this function.
This function is supported for compatibility.
Note that in this function, if query exists exactly at the end of the argument text, it is not regarded as being followed by an empty string, and no empty string is stored in the last element of the returned array.This behavior differs from splitText, but it will not be changed in order to preserve compatibility.
function20.parameters:
(string type) text : The target string. 
(string type) query : The delimiter string, which is not included in the contents of the resulting string array.
function20.returnValue: (string[] type) An array storing the split results.

function21.name: splitPattern
function21.declaration: string[ ] splitPattern( string text, string query )
function21.description:
Splits a string at positions matching a specific regular expression, and returns the result as an array.
Currently, the newer splitText function is supported and provides the same or more advanced functionality.For example, splitText(text, query, ALL_PATTERN) gives the same result as this function.
This function is supported for compatibility.
Note that in this function, if query exists exactly at the end of the argument text, it is not regarded as being followed by an empty string, and no empty string is stored in the last element of the returned array.This behavior differs from splitText, but it will not be changed in order to preserve compatibility.
function21.parameters:
(string type) text : The target string. 
(string type) query : The delimiter regular expression, which is not included in the contents of the resulting string array.
function21.returnValue: (string[] type) An array storing the split results.

function22.name: replace
function22.declaration: string replace( string text, string oldText, string newText )
function22.description:
Returns a string in which all occurrences of a substring contained in a string are replaced with another substring.
Currently, the newer replaceText function is supported and provides the same or more advanced functionality.For example, replaceText(text, query, ALL) gives the same result as this function.
This function is supported for compatibility.
function22.parameters:
(string type) text : The target string. 
(string type) oldText : The substring before replacement. 
(string type) newText : The substring after replacement.

function23.name: replacePattern
function23.declaration: string replacePattern( string text, string oldText, string newText )
function23.description:
Returns a string in which all ranges matching a regular expression in a string are replaced with another substring.
Currently, the newer replaceText function is supported and provides the same or more advanced functionality.For example, replaceText(text, query, ALL_PATTERN) gives the same result as this function.
This function is supported for compatibility.
function23.parameters:
(string type) text : The target string. 
(string type) oldText : The regular expression for replacement targets. 
(string type) newText : The substring after replacement.

function24.name: replaceFirst
function24.declaration: string replaceFirst( string text, string oldText, string newText )
function24.description:
Returns a string in which only the first occurrence of a substring contained in a string is replaced with another substring.
Currently, the newer replaceText function is supported and provides the same or more advanced functionality.For example, replaceText(text, query, FIRST) gives the same result as this function.
This function is supported for compatibility.
function24.parameters:
(string type) text : The target string. 
(string type) oldText : The substring before replacement. 
(string type) newText : The substring after replacement.

function25.name: replaceFirstPattern
function25.declaration: string replaceFirstPattern( string text, string oldText, string newText )
function25.description:
Returns a string in which only the first range matching a regular expression in a string is replaced with another substring.
Currently, the newer replaceText function is supported and provides the same or more advanced functionality.For example, replaceText(text, query, FIRST_PATTERN) gives the same result as this function.
This function is supported for compatibility.
function25.parameters:
(string type) text : The target string. 
(string type) oldText : The regular expression for replacement targets. 
(string type) newText : The substring after replacement.

function26.name: replaceFirst
function26.declaration: string replaceFirst( string text, string oldText, string newText, int start )
function26.description:
Returns a string in which a substring is replaced with another substring.If multiple matching substrings exist, only the first one at or after the index specified by the argument start is replaced.
Currently, the newer replaceText function is supported and provides the same or more advanced functionality.For example, replaceText(text, query, start, FIRST) gives the same result as this function.
This function is supported for compatibility.
function26.parameters:
(string type) text : The target string. 
(string type) oldText : The substring before replacement. 
(string type) newText : The substring after replacement. 
(int type) start : The start index of the search range.

function27.name: replaceFirstPattern
function27.declaration: string replaceFirstPattern( string text, string oldText, string newText, int start )
function27.description:
Returns a string in which a range matching a regular expression is replaced with another substring.If multiple matching ranges exist, only the first one at or after the index specified by the argument start is replaced.
Currently, the newer replaceText function is supported and provides the same or more advanced functionality.For example, replaceText(text, query, start, FIRST_PATTERN) gives the same result as this function.
This function is supported for compatibility.
function27.parameters:
(string type) text : The target string. 
(string type) oldText : The regular expression for replacement targets. 
(string type) newText : The substring after replacement. 
(int type) start : The start index of the search range.

function28.name: substring
function28.declaration: string substring( string text, int start, int end )
function28.description:
Returns the substring in the specified index range within a string.
Currently, the newer cropText function is supported and provides the same functionality.For example, cropText(text, start, end) gives the same result as this function.
This function is supported for compatibility.
function28.parameters:
(string type) text : The target string. 
(int type) start : The start index of the range. 
(int type) end : The end index of the range plus 1.

function29.name: lengthOf
function29.description:
Returns the number of characters in a string.
Currently, the newer countText function is supported and provides the same functionality.For example, countText(text) gives the same result as this function.
This function is supported for compatibility.

function30.name: startsWith
function30.declaration: bool startsWith( string text, string word )
function30.description:
Determines whether a string starts with the specified substring.
Currently, the newer checkText function is supported and provides the same functionality.For example, checkText(text, word, START) gives the same result as this function.
This function is supported for compatibility.
function30.parameters:
(string type) text : The target string. 
(string type) word : The substring.
function30.returnValue: (bool type) Returns true if the string starts with the specified substring, or false otherwise.

function31.name: endsWith
function31.declaration: bool endsWith( string text, string word )
function31.description:
Determines whether a string ends with the specified substring.
Currently, the newer checkText function is supported and provides the same functionality.For example, checkText(text, word, END) gives the same result as this function.
This function is supported for compatibility.
function31.parameters:
(string type) text : The target string. 
(string type) word : The substring.
function31.returnValue: (bool type) Returns true if the string ends with the specified substring, or false otherwise.

function32.name: match
function32.declaration: string match( string text, string pattern )
function32.description:
Use of this API is not recommended.
Currently, the newer extractText function is supported and provides the same functionality.For example, extractText(text, pattern, FIRST_PATTERN) gives the same result as this function.
This function is supported for compatibility.

function33.name: match
function33.declaration: string match( string text, string pattern, int start )
function33.description:
Use of this API is not recommended.
Currently, the newer extractText function is supported and provides the same functionality.For example, extractText(text, pattern, start, FIRST_PATTERN) gives the same result as this function.
This function is supported for compatibility.

function34.name: matchFull
function34.declaration: bool matchFull( string text, string pattern )
function34.description:
Determines whether an entire string matches a regular expression.
Currently, the newer checkText function is supported and provides the same functionality.For example, checkText(text, word, FULL_PATTERN) gives the same result as this function.
This function is supported for compatibility.
function34.parameters:
(string type) text : The target string. 
(string type) pattern : The regular expression.
function34.returnValue: (bool type) Returns true if the entire string matches the regular expression, or false otherwise.

function35.name: matchFirst
function35.declaration: string matchFirst( string text, string pattern )
function35.description:
Extracts and returns the first part matching a regular expression from a string.
Currently, the newer extractText function is supported and provides the same functionality.For example, extractText(text, pattern, FIRST_PATTERN) gives the same result as this function.
This function is supported for compatibility.
function35.parameters:
(string type) text : The target string. 
(string type) pattern : The regular expression.
function35.returnValue: (string type) The substring matching the regular expression.

function36.name: matchFirst
function36.declaration: string matchFirst( string text, string pattern, int start )
function36.description:
Extracts and returns a part matching a regular expression from a string.If multiple matching parts exist, only the first one at or after the index specified by the argument start is selected.
Currently, the newer extractText function is supported and provides the same functionality.For example, extractText(text, pattern, start, FIRST_PATTERN) gives the same result as this function.
This function is supported for compatibility.
function36.parameters:
(string type) text : The target string. 
(string type) pattern : The regular expression. 
(int type) start : The start index of the search range.
function36.returnValue: (string type) The substring matching the regular expression.

function37.name: matchLast
function37.declaration: string matchLast( string text, string pattern )
function37.description:
Extracts and returns the last part matching a regular expression from a string.
Currently, the newer extractText function is supported and provides the same functionality.For example, extractText(text, pattern, LAST_PATTERN) gives the same result as this function.
This function is supported for compatibility.
function37.parameters:
(string type) text : The target string. 
(string type) pattern : The regular expression.
function37.returnValue: (string type) The substring matching the regular expression.

function38.name: matchLast
function38.declaration: string matchLast( string text, string pattern, int end )
function38.description:
Extracts and returns a part matching a regular expression from a string.If multiple matching parts exist, only the last one before the position of the index specified by the argument end is selected.
Currently, the newer extractText function is supported and provides the same functionality.For example, extractText(text, pattern, 0, end, LAST_PATTERN) gives the same result as this function.
This function is supported for compatibility.
function38.parameters:
(string type) text : The target string. 
(string type) pattern : The regular expression. 
(int type) end : The end index of the search range.
function38.returnValue: (string type) The substring matching the regular expression.

function39.name: indexOf
function39.declaration: int indexOf( string text, string word )
function39.description:
Obtains the position of the specified substring in a string.If multiple occurrences exist, the first one is selected. If none exists, -1 is returned.
Currently, the newer findText function is supported and provides the same functionality.For example, findText(text, word, FIRST) gives the same result as this function.(However, if there is no match, an array with zero elements is returned.)
To determine whether a string contains a substring, you can use something like 0 < countText(text, word, ALL).
This function is supported for compatibility.
function39.parameters:
(string type) text : The target string. 
(string type) word : The substring.
function39.returnValue: (int type) The position index of the substring.

function40.name: indexOf
function40.declaration: int indexOf( string text, string word, int start )
function40.description:
Obtains the position of the specified substring in a string.If multiple occurrences exist, the first one at or after the index specified by the argument start is selected. If none exists, -1 is returned.
Currently, the newer findText function is supported and provides the same functionality.For example, findText(text, word, start, FIRST) gives the same result as this function.(However, if there is no match, an array with zero elements is returned.)
To determine whether a substring exists at or after the specified position in a string, you can use something like 0 < countText(text, word, start, ALL).
This function is supported for compatibility.
function40.parameters:
(string type) text : The target string. 
(string type) word : The substring. 
(int type) start : The start index of the search range.
function40.returnValue: (int type) The position index of the substring.

function41.name: indexOfPattern
function41.declaration: string indexOfPattern( string text, string word )
function41.description:
Obtains the position of the specified regular expression match in a string.If multiple matches exist, the first one is selected. If none exists, -1 is returned.
Currently, the newer findText function is supported and provides the same functionality.For example, findText(text, word, FIRST_PATTERN) gives the same result as this function.(However, if there is no match, an array with zero elements is returned.)
This function is supported for compatibility.
function41.parameters:
(string type) text : The target string. 
(string type) word : The regular expression.
function41.returnValue: (string type) The position index of the matched part.

function42.name: indexOfPattern
function42.declaration: string indexOfPattern( string text, string word, int start )
function42.description:
Obtains the position of the specified regular expression match in a string.If multiple matches exist, the first one at or after the index specified by the argument start is selected. If none exists, -1 is returned.
Currently, the newer findText function is supported and provides the same functionality.For example, findText(text, word, start, FIRST_PATTERN) gives the same result as this function.(However, if there is no match, an array with zero elements is returned.)
This function is supported for compatibility.
function42.parameters:
(string type) text : The target string. 
(string type) word : The regular expression. 
(int type) start : The start index of the search range.
function42.returnValue: (string type) The position index of the matched part.

function43.name: lastIndexOf
function43.declaration: int lastIndexOf( string text, string word )
function43.description:
Obtains the position of the specified substring in a string.If multiple occurrences exist, the last one is selected. If none exists, -1 is returned.
Currently, the newer findText function is supported and provides the same functionality.For example, findText(text, word, LAST) gives the same result as this function.(However, if there is no match, an array with zero elements is returned.)
This function is supported for compatibility.
function43.parameters:
(string type) text : The target string. 
(string type) word : The substring.
function43.returnValue: (int type) The position index of the substring.

function44.name: lastIndexOf
function44.declaration: int lastIndexOf( string text, string word, int end )
function44.description:
Obtains the position of the specified substring in a string.If multiple occurrences exist, the last one before or at the index specified by the argument end is selected. If none exists, -1 is returned.
Currently, the newer findText function is supported and provides the same functionality.For example, findText(text, word, 0, end, LAST) gives the same result as this function.(However, if there is no match, an array with zero elements is returned.)
This function is supported for compatibility.
function44.parameters:
(string type) text : The target string. 
(string type) word : The substring. 
(int type) end : The end index of the search range.
function44.returnValue: (int type) The position index of the substring.

function45.name: lastIndexOfPattern
function45.declaration: string lastIndexOfPattern( string text, string word )
function45.description:
Obtains the position of the specified regular expression match in a string.If multiple matches exist, the last one is selected. If none exists, -1 is returned.
Currently, the newer findText function is supported and provides the same functionality.For example, findText(text, word, LAST_PATTERN) gives the same result as this function.(However, if there is no match, an array with zero elements is returned.)
This function is supported for compatibility.
function45.parameters:
(string type) text : The target string. 
(string type) word : The regular expression.
function45.returnValue: (string type) The position index of the matched part.

function46.name: lastIndexOfPattern
function46.declaration: string lastIndexOfPattern( string text, string word, int end )
function46.description:
Obtains the position of the specified regular expression match in a string.If multiple matches exist, the last one before or at the index specified by the argument end is selected. If none exists, -1 is returned.
Currently, the newer findText function is supported and provides the same functionality.For example, findText(text, word, 0, end, LAST_PATTERN) gives the same result as this function.(However, if there is no match, an array with zero elements is returned.)
This function is supported for compatibility.
function46.parameters:
(string type) text : The target string. 
(string type) word : The regular expression. 
(int type) end : The end index of the search range.
function46.returnValue: (string type) The position index of the matched part.

function47.name: charAt
function47.declaration: string charAt( string text, int index )
function47.description:
Returns the character at the specified index in a string.
Currently, the newer atText function is supported and provides the same functionality.For example, atText(text, index) gives the same result as this function.
This function is supported for compatibility.
function47.parameters:
(string type) text : The target string. 
(int type) index : The character index.

function48.name: tokenize
function48.description: Splits a string into tokens separated by spaces, tabs, or line breaks, and returns them as an array.
